text: Project opened: Orchestration console
Task added: File protocol + console_store.py
Task 'File protocol + console_store.py': status=done (11 plumbing tests green, incl. concurrent feed-seq)
Task added: console CLI (the action surface)
Task 'console CLI (the action surface)': status=done (argparse over the store; used to build this very board)
Task added: console_web.py dashboard + writeback
Task 'console_web.py dashboard + writeback': status=in-review (built; being clicked through in the browser now)
Task added: orchestration-console SKILL.md
Task added: Hook the console into implement-feature / queue-implement-feature
Task 'Hook the console into implement-feature / queue-implement-feature': status=blocked, blocked_on=status-md-migration
Decision needs you: Retire scratch/STATUS.md, or generate it as a fallback during rollout?
Store + CLI done and tested (11 green). Web app built, clicking through it now. One decision waiting on you: how to migrate STATUS.md.
Decision needs you: Throwaway: does UI writeback work?
Answered 'Throwaway: does UI writeback work?': yes, writeback works end to end
Decided (locked): Throwaway: does UI writeback work?
Decision dropped: Throwaway: does UI writeback work? (throwaway test cleanup)
Task 'console_web.py dashboard + writeback': status=done (renders + writeback verified via curl; page snapshot sent to user)
Task 'orchestration-console SKILL.md': status=done (written at .claude/skills/orchestration-console/SKILL.md)
Task 'Hook the console into implement-feature / queue-implement-feature': status=in-review (hooks added to implement-feature + queue-implement-feature; STATUS.md migration still open)
Task added: TESTING.md + skill conformance check
Task 'TESTING.md + skill conformance check': status=running (3-layer recipe written; running the cold conformance check now)
Skill + skill-edits landed. Testing recipe written. Running a cold skill-conformance check (fresh sub-agent, skill only) to prove the agent uses the console right.

Task 'TESTING.md + skill conformance check': status=done (Layer 1: 11 tests green. Layer 2: writeback verified. Layer 3a: cold conformance check PASSED (fresh agent produced a correct board from the skill alone).)

Built and tested. Skill-conformance check passed: a fresh agent given only the skill produced a correct board (3 tasks with right statuses, blocked-on wired, one well-formed decision). One decision waiting on you: the STATUS.md migration.
Answered 'Retire scratch/STATUS.md, or generate it as a fallback during rollout?': let's retrire it
Decided (locked): Retire scratch/STATUS.md, or generate it as a fallback during rollout?
Task 'Hook the console into implement-feature / queue-implement-feature': status=done (STATUS.md decision locked (retire). Wording updated across the two orchestration skills.)

Closed the loop: you answered the STATUS.md decision in the UI, I picked it up via 'console pending', applied it (retire STATUS.md, no fallback), and locked it. This whole exchange happened without a chat round-trip on the decision itself.
Thread opened: How do I get my other running agent sessions to use this?

Reply on 'How do I get my other running agent sessions to use this?': Yes, finished: skill written, both orchestration skills wired, conformance test passed, and we ran the loop live. Answer above. Open question still on the table: want me to mirror the skill into .agents/skills/ for Codex, and commit to a GitButler lane?
Thread opened: Why do tasks show placeholder Context/Explanations?

Reply on 'Why do tasks show placeholder Context/Explanations?': Yes. Fixed the scaffold so empty tasks never show fake placeholder prose again. Added a regression test (test_task_no_placeholder_prose).
Thread opened: It's not only tasks: add conversation threads I can post into
Reply on 'It's not only tasks: add conversation threads I can post into': Built and tested (17 tests green). This thread you're reading is itself one of the threads, seeded to dogfood the feature.
Task 'Hook the console into implement-feature / queue-implement-feature': status=done (STATUS.md decision applied everywhere.)
Added conversation threads + per-task messages + an 'ask a question' box in the UI. Seeded the real threads from our conversation so you can see the feature holding our discussion.
Thread opened: Can I reorder the dashboard sections?
Message on 'How do I get my other running agent sessions to use this?': please also mirror it for Codex
Message on task 'console_web.py dashboard + writeback': can we add keyboard shortcuts later?

Reply on 'How do I get my other running agent sessions to use this?': Yes, I can mirror it into .agents/skills/orchestration-console/ for Codex auto-discovery. Say the word and I'll add it in the same commit.

Reply on 'Can I reorder the dashboard sections?': Yes, the section order is one template in console_web.py. Needs-you first is deliberate (most urgent), but I can reorder Threads/Tasks however you like.
Thread 'Can I reorder the dashboard sections?': resolved
Task 'console_web.py dashboard + writeback': updated (Noted: keyboard shortcuts as a later enhancement (logged, not scheduled).)
Message on 'Why do tasks show placeholder Context/Explanations?': thx. close
Message on 'How do I get my other running agent sessions to use this?': give me the short instruction to paste
Note: when i add a note/answer to a thread I dont see it in the UI, and I don't see that it is waiting for you or whether you read it
Message on 'Why do tasks show placeholder Context/Explanations?': cool close.
Note: would be nice to be able to close threads with a button instead of writing close each time

Note: mayeb in the ui being able to collapse the what s new somehow would be helpful and easy way to reach quick note if needed (maybe a button above with dropdown or something better than scrolling each tim eto the end
Note: same for theread, ability to collapse expand one or all would be helpful to navigate

Reply on 'How do I get my other running agent sessions to use this?': Paste this into each running session: >> Track this work in the orchestration console. Read .claude/skills/orchestration-console/SKILL.md and follow it. Open a project (short id), root docs/design/agent-workflows/scratch/console. Every topic I raise = a thread; every sub-agent = a task; every state change = a console message. At the start of each turn run: uv run docs/design/agent-workflows/projects/orchestration-console/tool/console.py pending --project <id>, and act on my posts first. <<
Reply on 'Why do tasks show placeholder Context/Explanations?': Closing. Scaffold fixed, regression test added.
Thread 'Why do tasks show placeholder Context/Explanations?': resolved

Built the UI feedback from your notes: 'waiting for agent' badges on threads/tasks, a Resolve button, collapsible threads (expand/collapse all) + a sticky toolbar, and a collapsible 'What's new' with quick-jump to Ask/Note. Replied to your two threads.
Thread 'It's not only tasks: add conversation threads I can post into': resolved

Mirrored the skill into .agents/skills/ (symlink) so Codex auto-discovers it. Backed everything up (code + skill + edited skills + console data) to ~/backups/orchestration-console-2026-07-01/ with a RESTORE.md. Not committed, per your call.
Redesigned the UI: light + dark themes with a toggle (top-right, remembers your choice, defaults to system), cleaner typography and spacing, softer cards, and readable status colors in both themes.
Thread 'How do I get my other running agent sessions to use this?': resolved

Shipped: thread states (waiting/investigating/resolved), readable markdown messages+summaries, cross-project backlog with Save-to-backlog, and theme toggle. Skill + design doc updated (new states, backlog, writing voice, delegate upkeep to a cheap Sonnet subagent). Migrated existing data. 21 tests green.
Message on 'How do I get my other running agent sessions to use this?': test: does incremental poll pick this up?
Reply on 'How do I get my other running agent sessions to use this?': (test acknowledged)
Added a live protocol: poll/watch (incremental, cursor-based) + a comms sub-agent pattern so the orchestrator keeps working and only gets relayed user messages. Skill updated. 25 tests green.
